scope: Targeted clarity + duplicate audit for สวัสดี cluster, ไม่ / ไหม / มั้ย homophone cluster, and ไม่เป็นไร cluster (per lesson-UX brief).

rules.deletion: No card was deleted. Even high-confidence duplicates retained because users may have SRS progress on them.
rules.thaiText: No Thai text was guessed. Anything uncertain was flagged for native review.

changed.noteAfter: General greeting. Means 'hello' in most contexts and can also be used for 'goodbye'. Not time-of-day specific — works morning, afternoon, or night. Add ครับ (male) or ค่ะ (female) for the polite forms.
changed.reason: Bug-fix: card claimed only 'goodbye'. สวัสดี is the canonical general greeting (hello AND goodbye).

changed.thai: สวัสดีครับ
changed.noteBefore: Universal greeting. Time of day does not matter.
changed.noteAfter: Polite greeting used by male speakers — works for hello AND goodbye, any time of day. ครับ is the male polite particle. Female speakers say สวัสดีค่ะ.
changed.reason: Make the male/female pairing explicit and learnable.

changed.noteAfter: Polite greeting used by female speakers. ค่ะ is the female polite particle. Works for hello and goodbye in any context. Male speakers use สวัสดีครับ.
changed.reason: Symmetric framing with the male card (#310).

changed.noteBefore: Goes BEFORE the verb. Falling tone — careful:mǎi (rising) is a question marker.
changed.noteAfter: Used BEFORE verbs/adjectives to make them negative: 'mâi gin' = don't eat. Falling tone. Do NOT confuse with ไหม / มั้ย (rising tone) — those are question particles that go at the END of a sentence.
changed.reason: Avoid ไม่ vs ไหม / มั้ย confusion (per brief).

changed.noteBefore: Tack onto end:"ao mǎi?" = "want?" Rising tone.
changed.noteAfter: Tacks onto the END of a sentence to make it a yes/no question: 'ao mǎi?' = 'want it?'. Rising tone. In casual speech you'll also hear มั้ย (mái) — same job, more spoken. Do NOT confuse with ไม่ (mâi, falling) which means 'no/not'.
changed.reason: Distinguish the question particle from negation; introduce มั้ย as a casual variant.

changed.thai: ไม่เป็นไรครับ
changed.noteAfter: Polite male-speaker form of ไม่เป็นไร — the unofficial Thai national motto. Means 'no worries', 'you're welcome', 'it's fine', or 'never mind' depending on context. Female speakers say ไม่เป็นไรค่ะ.
changed.reason: Per brief: explicitly classify as polite male-speaker form, list common English shades.

changed.thai: ไม่เป็นไรครับ
changed.noteAfter: Polite male-speaker form of ไม่เป็นไร. Reintroduced at Stage 4 as a full polite sentence — same phrase as the Stage 1 greeting (card #310 territory) but reframed in the politeness category.
changed.reason: Document that the Stage 4 appearance is intentional re-exposure, not a mistake.

changed.noteAfter: Spoken/casual variant of ไหม (mǎi). Same job — tacks onto the END of a sentence to make a yes/no question — but you'll hear มั้ย more in everyday speech. Do NOT confuse with ไม่ (mâi, falling) which means 'no/not'.
changed.reason: Ensure question-particle usage is unambiguous; cross-reference ไหม and warn vs ไม่.

flagged.thai: สวัสดีค่ะ / สวัสดีครับ
flagged.reason: Combines both gender forms with a slash in a single card. Stage 2 placement overlaps with the stage-1 cards (#310, #4485). Risky to auto-delete because users may have SRS progress on it.
flagged.recommendation: Delete (covered by #310 + #4485) OR convert to a single polite-particle teaching card.

flagged.englishBefore: It's okay. / It's fine. / It's no problem. / It's all right. / You're welcome. / Never mind.
flagged.englishAfter: It's okay. / It's fine. / It's no problem. / It's all right. / You're welcome. / Never mind.
flagged.reason: English enumerates many meanings — readable but unusual vs the rest of the deck. Trim to the 2-3 most common to reduce cognitive load.

flagged.englishBefore: variant of มั้ย/ไหม (question)
flagged.englishAfter: variant of มั้ย/ไหม (question)
flagged.reason: Three spellings (ไหม, มั้ย, ไม๊) all teach the same particle. Confirm with native speaker whether ไม๊ is common enough to warrant a card, or whether it should be a note on #118 / #5704 instead.

flagged.thai: ไม่เป็นไรหรอก
flagged.reason: Pedagogically distinct casual variant. English could be richer (note what หรอก adds — mild reassurance / dismissive softening).

Same-romanization homophone with ไม่ / ไหม / มั้ย / ไม๊. Need a native-confirmed note clarifying that ไหม้ is a completely different word (verb, falling tone, mai-tho tone mark) and that the romanization collision is orthographic confusion to be aware of.

members.thai: สวัสดีครับ
สวัสดีค่ะ / สวัสดีครับ
ไม่เป็นไรครับ
ไม่เป็นไรครับ
ไม่เป็นไรหรอก

clusters.name: ไม่ / ไหม / มั้ย homophone cluster
